input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Lighting/Light/Lights`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Lighting/Light/Lights` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.2.1
            TITLE: harness definitions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.1: Harness Definitions
T.2.1 Harness Definitions 
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine 
Belt.  
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.  
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.   
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder 
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

RULE: ic.5.4.6
            TITLE: the sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.4.6: The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,
IC.5.4.6 The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank, 
the other to visually verify fill) without the need of assistance (artificial lighting, magnifiers, 
etc) or the need to remove any parts (body panels, etc).

RULE: ev.5.9.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must include a tractive systems active light (tsal) that must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.9.1: The vehicle must include a Tractive Systems Active Light (TSAL) that must:
EV.5.9.1 The vehicle must include a Tractive Systems Active Light (TSAL) that must: 
a. Illuminate when the GLV System is energized to indicate the status of the Tractive 
System 
b. Be directly controlled by the voltage present in the Tractive System using hard wired 
electronics.  Software control is not permitted. 
c. Not perform any other functions.

RULE: t.1.5.1
            TITLE: the driver’s seat must be protected by one of the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.5.1: The Driver’s Seat must be protected by one of the following:
T.1.5.1 The Driver’s Seat must be protected by one of the following: 
a. In side view, the lowest point of any Driver’s Seat must be no lower than the upper 
surface of the lowest structural tube or equivalent 
b. A longitudinal tube (or tubes) that meets the requirements for Side Impact tubing 
(F.3.2.1.e), passing underneath the lowest point of the Driver Seat.

RULE: t.1.6.3
            TITLE: the design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.3: The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples
T.1.6.3 The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples 
include but are not limited to:  exhaust pipe, coolant hose/tube, Accumulator Container) and 
the panel that the driver could contact (seat or floor): 
a. Conduction Isolation by one of the following: 
• No direct contact between the heat source and the panel 
• A heat resistant, conduction isolation material with a minimum thickness of 8 mm 
between the heat source and the panel  
b. Convection Isolation by a minimum air gap of 25 mm between the heat source and the 
panel 
c. Radiation Isolation by one of the following: 
• A solid metal heat shield with a minimum thickness of 0.4 mm 
• Reflective foil or tape when combined with conduction insulation

RULE: t.3.4.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must have a brake light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.4.1: The vehicle must have a Brake Light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.
T.3.4.1 The vehicle must have a Brake Light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.

RULE: ev.7.6.5
            TITLE: if the imd detects one or more of the conditions of ev.7.6.4 above the imd must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.6.5: If the IMD detects one or more of the conditions of EV.7.6.4 above the IMD must:
EV.7.6.5 If the IMD detects one or more of the conditions of EV.7.6.4 above the IMD must: 
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit  EV.7.2.2 
b. Turn on the IMD Indicator Light.  The light must stay on until the IMD is reset EV.7.2.3

RULE: ev.4.9.1
            TITLE: every housing or enclosure containing parts of the tractive system other than motor

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.9.1: Every housing or enclosure containing parts of the Tractive System other than Motor
EV.4.9.1 Every housing or enclosure containing parts of the Tractive System other than Motor 
housings, must be labelled with the:   
a. Symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012  (triangle with black lightning bolt on yellow 
background)  
b. Text “High Voltage” if the voltage meets T.9.1.1

RULE: ev.7.3.5
            TITLE: if the ams detects one or more of the conditions of ev.7.3.4 above, the ams must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.3.5: If the AMS detects one or more of the conditions of EV.7.3.4 above, the AMS must:
EV.7.3.5 If the AMS detects one or more of the conditions of EV.7.3.4 above, the AMS must:  
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit  EV.7.2.2 
b. Turn on the AMS Indicator Light.  The light must stay on until the AMS is reset EV.7.2.3

RULE: ev.6.4.4
            TITLE: information about the electrical connections supporting the high current path must be

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.4.4: Information about the electrical connections supporting the high current path must be
EV.6.4.4 Information about the electrical connections supporting the high current path must be 
available at Electrical Technical Inspection

RULE: ev.5.9.2
            TITLE: the tsal may be composed of multiple lights inside a single housing

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.9.2: The TSAL may be composed of multiple lights inside a single housing
EV.5.9.2 The TSAL may be composed of multiple lights inside a single housing

RULE: d.14.3.4
            TITLE: team may be dnf or dq for:

            CONTENT:
            rule D.14.3.4: Team may be DNF or DQ for:
D.14.3.4 Team may be DNF or DQ for:  
a. Multiple violations involving safety, environment, or performance advantage  
b. A single substantial violation

RULE: t.3.4.4
            TITLE: if a single line of leds is used, the minimum length is 150 mm.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.4.4: If a single line of LEDs is used, the minimum length is 150 mm.
T.3.4.4 If a single line of LEDs is used, the minimum length is 150 mm.

RULE: t.1.8.1
            TITLE: requirement

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.1: Requirement
T.1.8.1 Requirement 
A Firewall(s) must separate the driver compartment and any portion of the Driver Harness 
from:  
a. All components of the Fuel System, the engine oil, the liquid cooling systems, any lithium 
batteries 
b. (EV only) All Tractive System components other than Outboard Wheel Motors EV.4.1.3 
where mounted at the wheels or on the front control arms

RULE: ev.6.3.4
            TITLE: any tractive system wiring that runs outside of electrical enclosures:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.3.4: Any Tractive System wiring that runs outside of electrical enclosures:
EV.6.3.4 Any Tractive System wiring that runs outside of electrical enclosures:  
a. Must meet one of: 
• Enclosed in separate orange nonconductive conduit  
• Use an orange shielded cable.  
b. Must meet one of: 
• Run in a fully enclosed container.  Bodywork is not an enclosure.   
• The conduit or shielded cable is securely anchored at each end to allow it to 
withstand a force of 200 N without straining the cable end crimp 
c. Any shielded cable must have the shield grounded.

RULE: d.12.6.1
            TITLE: a green flag or light signal will give the approval to begin the run

            CONTENT:
            rule D.12.6.1: A Green Flag or light signal will give the approval to begin the run
D.12.6.1 A Green Flag or light signal will give the approval to begin the run

RULE: t.3.4.3
            TITLE: when led lights are used without a diffuser, they must not be more than 20 mm apart.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.4.3: When LED lights are used without a diffuser, they must not be more than 20 mm apart.
T.3.4.3 When LED lights are used without a diffuser, they must not be more than 20 mm apart.

RULE: ev.6.3.5
            TITLE: wiring that is not part of the tractive system must not use orange wiring or conduit.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.6.3.5: Wiring that is not part of the Tractive System must not use orange wiring or conduit.
EV.6.3.5 Wiring that is not part of the Tractive System must not use orange wiring or conduit.

RULE: t.1.6.1
            TITLE: when seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.1: When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to
T.1.6.1 When seated in the normal driving position, sufficient heat insulation must be provided to 
ensure that the driver will not contact any metal or other materials which may become heated 
to a surface temperature above 60°C.

RULE: f.11.1.3
            TITLE: tractive system (ev.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in f.11.1.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.1.3: Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2
F.11.1.3 Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2 
above must be contained inside one or both of: 
• The Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13 
• Structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection
